title: React Memoization Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of caching results, React.memo, useMemo, and useCallback hooks.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary goal of memoization in React?
options: To fetch data from a server faster.
To avoid unnecessary re-renders of components and recalculations of expensive functions.
To provide a replacement for CSS styles.

questions.question: Which hook should you use to memoize a computed value between renders?
Which hook returns a memoized version of a callback function?
What is React.memo() used for?
options: A higher-order component to prevent a component from re-rendering if its props don't change.

questions.question: Should you always use memoization for every single component and function for the best performance?
options: No, it has overhead and should be used only for expensive calculations or components that re-render frequently.
Yes, but only in ES6 or later.
Only if you return them with an ID.
